name: Level Up (Ages 10-12)
description: Advanced skills for independence, advocacy, and social navigation

activities.title: I Need a Break Because...
activities.target: State reason for needing break in complete sentence
activities.script_parent: 'Why do you need a break?' Offer: tired, frustrated, or need quiet. Build to them stating reason.
activities.script_child: I need a break because I'm feeling ___.
activities.success_criteria: States reason 3/5 break requests
activities.generalization: At school, during homework, in overwhelming situations

activities.title: Can We Make a Plan?
activities.target: Request collaborative problem-solving with adult
activities.script_parent: Teach: 'Can we make a plan for [problem]?' Practice when calm. Honor the request!
activities.script_child: Can we talk about how to handle this?
activities.success_criteria: Initiates plan-making 2/4 appropriate opportunities
activities.generalization: When facing difficult task, conflict, or challenge

variants.phrase: Can we make a plan for finishing this?
Can we talk about what's changing?
Can we figure out what to do?

activities.title: Email the Teacher (Template)
activities.target: Write respectful email request using template
activities.script_parent: Use template: 'Hi [teacher], I wanted to ask about [topic]. Thank you, [name].' Review before sending.
activities.script_child: Let's write an email together. Use polite words.
activities.success_criteria: Completes appropriate email with partial template or less
activities.generalization: For homework questions, absence explanation, clarification

variants.template: I'd like to turn in [assignment]. Thank you.

activities.title: Explaining My Accommodation
activities.target: Explain one accommodation/support to peer in simple terms
activities.script_parent: 'I use [tool/accommodation] because it helps me [reason].' Keep it simple and positive. Practice!
activities.script_child: This helps me focus better. It's just how I work best.
activities.success_criteria: Explains accommodation positively 2/3 relevant situations
activities.generalization: When peers ask, starting new class, meeting new people

variants.script: This helps me focus on the teacher
I use these when it gets too loud for me
Sometimes I need a quick break to do my best work

activities.title: Saying No Safely
activities.target: Decline peer pressure with assertive but friendly refusal
activities.script_parent: Teach: 'No thanks,' 'I'm good,' 'Not for me.' Practice and role-play. Discuss when to tell adult.
activities.script_child: It's okay to say no. You can say 'no thanks' and stay friendly.
activities.success_criteria: Uses refusal in role-play 4/5 times
activities.mastery_rule: Role-play mastery (real situations are harder to measure!)
activities.generalization: Role-play various scenarios, discuss real situations as they arise

variants.script: No thanks, I don't want to

activities.title: Joining a Group
activities.target: Use 3-step approach to join ongoing group activity
activities.script_parent: Steps: 1) Observe, 2) Move closer, 3) Comment or ask. 'What are you guys doing?' or 'That looks cool!'
activities.script_child: Watch first, then move close, then say something friendly.
activities.success_criteria: Attempts all 3 steps in role-play or real situation 3/5 times
activities.generalization: Lunch, recess, before class, extracurriculars

activities.title: Topic Shift Kindly
activities.target: Recognize when others are bored and use transition phrase to change topic
activities.script_parent: Watch for: short answers, looking away, changing subject. Then: 'Anyway, what about you?' or 'Speaking of...'
activities.script_child: Notice if they seem interested. If not, you can change topics.
activities.success_criteria: Recognizes boredom cue 3/5 conversations and attempts transition
activities.generalization: Peer conversations, family dinnertime, social situations

variants.transition: Anyway, what did you do today?

activities.title: Exit a Conversation
activities.target: Use polite exit phrase to end conversation
activities.script_parent: Teach: 'I gotta go,' 'See you later,' 'Nice talking to you!' Don't just walk away. Practice!
activities.script_child: Use a goodbye phrase before you leave a conversation.
activities.success_criteria: Uses exit phrase 4/5 conversation endings
activities.generalization: End of lunch, between classes, ending video call, leaving activity

activities.title: Reading Subtle Cues
activities.target: Identify 3 subtle social cues (tone, face, body language) from video/photo
activities.script_parent: Watch video. 'How does she feel?' Look at face, voice, body. 'She rolled her eyes—that means annoyed.'
activities.script_child: What do you notice about how they're acting? What might they be feeling?
activities.success_criteria: Identifies 2/3 cues with yes/no prompts or less
activities.generalization: Watch TV together, real-life coaching, social situations

activities.title: What Else Could They Mean?
activities.target: Generate alternative interpretation for ambiguous social situation
activities.script_parent: 'They didn't wave back. Maybe they didn't see you? Or maybe distracted?' Teach multiple possibilities.
activities.script_child: What's another reason that could have happened?
activities.success_criteria: Generates 2nd interpretation with 'or maybe' prompt 3/5 scenarios
activities.generalization: When misunderstandings happen, when feelings hurt, social coaching

variants.alt: tired or thinking about something else
didn't hear or didn't get it

activities.title: 3-Card Project Plan
activities.target: Break task into 3 steps using planning cards
activities.script_parent: Give 3 cards: 'First I'll...', 'Then I'll...', 'Finally I'll...' Help write steps. Do them in order!
activities.script_child: Let's break this big job into 3 steps.
activities.success_criteria: Completes 3-card plan with partial help, then follows it
activities.generalization: Homework, chores, projects, multi-step tasks

activities.title: Time Block a Day
activities.target: Create visual schedule with time blocks for after-school routine
activities.script_parent: Make schedule together: 3:00-3:30 snack, 3:30-4:30 homework, etc. Use timer to stick to it!
activities.script_child: Let's plan out your afternoon with times for each thing.
activities.success_criteria: Co-creates schedule and follows 3/4 blocks
activities.generalization: After school, weekend days, summer schedule

activities.title: Homework Start Ritual
activities.target: Complete 4-step ritual to begin homework independently
activities.script_parent: Create ritual: 1) Gather materials, 2) Set timer for 25 min, 3) Put phone away, 4) Start easiest task first.
activities.script_child: Follow your start ritual. It helps your brain get ready.
activities.success_criteria: Completes all 4 ritual steps with verbal reminder or less 4/5 days
activities.generalization: Daily homework time, test prep, project work

activities.title: Distraction Breaker Menu
activities.target: Choose and use strategy when distracted during work
activities.script_parent: Menu: take breath, stretch, refocus eyes on work, check off progress. When stuck: 'Check your menu!'
activities.script_child: If you notice you're distracted, pick a strategy from your menu.
activities.success_criteria: Notices distraction and uses strategy 3/5 work sessions
activities.generalization: Homework, reading, any focused work time

activities.title: Prioritize 3
activities.target: Rank 3 tasks by importance/urgency and do highest first
activities.script_parent: 'Which is most important? What's due tomorrow?' Help rank 1-2-3. Do #1 first!
activities.script_child: Look at all your tasks. Which one should you do first?
activities.success_criteria: Ranks tasks logically 3/5 times and starts with priority #1
activities.generalization: Multiple homework assignments, weekend chores, conflicting activities

variants.example: essay due tomorrow > project due Friday > reading
test prep > worksheet > optional extra credit

activities.title: Pack for Sports
activities.target: Pack sports bag with 5+ needed items using checklist
activities.script_parent: Checklist: uniform, shoes, water, snack, towel. Night before practice: 'Check your list!' Point. Fade prompts.
activities.script_child: Look at your checklist and pack everything you need.
activities.success_criteria: Packs 5/6 items with checklist only
activities.generalization: Sports, dance, scouts, any activity

activities.title: Kitchen Safety Intro
activities.target: State and follow 3 kitchen safety rules
activities.script_parent: Teach rules: 1) Ask before using stove/knife, 2) Pot handles turned in, 3) Wash hands first. Quiz daily!
activities.script_child: What are our kitchen safety rules?
activities.success_criteria: States all 3 rules and follows them during simple cooking
activities.generalization: All kitchen activities, at home and others' homes

activities.title: Microwave Snack Safely
activities.target: Independently heat safe snack in microwave following 5 steps
activities.script_parent: Steps: 1) Check if microwave-safe, 2) Set time, 3) Start, 4) Use oven mitt, 5) Stir and test temp. Supervise!
activities.script_child: Follow all the steps to heat your snack safely.
activities.success_criteria: Completes all 5 steps safely with checklist only
activities.generalization: After-school snacks, breakfast foods, allowed items

activities.title: Laundry Basics
activities.target: Sort clothes, start washer, move to dryer with supervision
activities.script_parent: Teach: Sort darks/lights. Add soap (show amount). Select cycle. Move when done. Supervise all steps!
activities.script_child: Let's do your laundry together. I'll guide you.
activities.success_criteria: Completes all steps with verbal prompts, no safety errors
activities.generalization: Weekly laundry day, as needed

activities.title: Bedtime Wind-Down Plan
activities.target: Create and follow personalized 30-min bedtime wind-down
activities.script_parent: Choose 4 calming activities for last 30 min: dim lights, hygiene, read, breathe. Must include screen-off rule.
activities.script_child: What helps you wind down? Let's make your plan.
activities.success_criteria: Co-creates plan and follows 4/5 nights
activities.generalization: Every night, travel nights with adaptation

activities.title: Body Map Check-In
activities.target: Identify where in body they feel emotions using body map
activities.script_parent: 'Where do you feel it in your body?' Use outline. 'Tight chest? Stomach flip? Hot face?' Build awareness.
activities.script_child: Show me on the body map where you feel that feeling.
activities.success_criteria: Identifies body location 3/5 emotional check-ins
activities.generalization: When emotional, during check-ins, therapy/counseling

activities.title: Name It to Tame It
activities.target: Label specific emotion and intensity level (1-10)
activities.script_parent: 'Name the feeling and rate it 1-10.' Naming reduces intensity! Track if it goes down after naming.
activities.script_child: What are you feeling, and how strong is it?
activities.success_criteria: Labels emotion and gives intensity 4/5 check-ins
activities.generalization: When upset, during reflection, daily check-ins

activities.title: Coping Menu Builder
activities.target: Create personalized menu with 5+ coping strategies
activities.script_parent: Pick what actually helps YOU: breathing, music, walk, journal, talk, fidget, etc. Test them! Keep what works.
activities.script_child: Let's make a menu of things that help when you're upset.
activities.success_criteria: Creates menu with 5+ strategies and tries 3+ over 2 weeks
activities.mastery_rule: Creates menu + uses strategies 60% of upset moments
activities.generalization: Home, school, anywhere emotions arise

variants.access: in backpack and posted at home

activities.title: Crisis Plan Rehearsal
activities.target: State and practice 3-step crisis plan when overwhelmed
activities.script_parent: Plan: 1) Remove to safe space, 2) Use top coping strategy, 3) Tell trusted adult. Practice when calm!
activities.script_child: If things feel really big, what's your plan?
activities.success_criteria: Recalls all 3 steps when asked, attempts plan 1+ time when overwhelmed
activities.mastery_rule: 100% recall, real-use is success whenever it happens
activities.generalization: School, home, community—everywhere

activities.title: Big Feelings Journal
activities.target: Complete structured journal entry after big emotion (what, why, helped, next time)
activities.script_parent: After you're calm: What happened? How'd you feel? What helped? What could you try next time? Write or talk it.
activities.script_child: Let's write about what happened. It helps your brain process.
activities.success_criteria: Completes all 4 parts of entry with partial prompts 3/5 incidents
activities.generalization: After any big emotion, as part of wind-down or therapy

activities.title: Pause Before Post
activities.target: Use 3-question check before posting/commenting online
activities.script_parent: Before you post: 1) Is it true? 2) Is it kind? 3) Could it hurt anyone? If no to any, don't post.
activities.script_child: Check these 3 questions before you hit send.
activities.success_criteria: Uses check before posting (parent monitors) 4/5 times
activities.generalization: All social media, group chats, comments

activities.title: Safe Replies
activities.target: Choose safe response to messages from strangers or uncomfortable requests
activities.script_parent: If someone you don't know messages, or asks for personal info: don't reply, screenshot, tell parent. Practice scenarios!
activities.script_child: What would be a safe way to handle this message?
activities.success_criteria: Chooses safe response in 4/5 role-play scenarios
activities.mastery_rule: Role-play mastery + tells parent about real suspicious messages
activities.generalization: All apps, games, platforms with messaging

variants.scenario: asks to move to different app

activities.title: Report vs Block
activities.target: Decide when to block, when to report, when to tell adult
activities.script_parent: Annoying but not harmful = block. Bullying or unsafe = report AND tell parent. Always okay to ask adult!
activities.script_child: Is this a block situation, a report situation, or both?
activities.success_criteria: Correctly categorizes 4/5 scenarios in role-play
activities.mastery_rule: 80% in practice + tells parent about real concerning content
activities.generalization: All platforms, apps, games

variants.action: don't interact, report, screenshot, tell parent immediately

activities.title: Group Chat Rules
activities.target: Follow 4 group chat etiquette rules
activities.script_parent: Rules: 1) Don't spam, 2) Stay on topic, 3) No sharing others' private stuff, 4) Leave if uncomfortable. Review weekly!
activities.script_child: What are our group chat rules?
activities.success_criteria: States all 4 rules and follows them (parent spot-checks) 4/5 checks
activities.generalization: All group chats, class chats, friend groups

activities.title: Screen Time Negotiation
activities.target: Negotiate screen time limits using calm discussion and compromise
activities.script_parent: Teach: 1) State what you want, 2) Listen to parent's concern, 3) Suggest compromise. Practice! Honor good-faith tries.
activities.script_child: Let's talk about screen time. Can you suggest a fair plan?
activities.success_criteria: Attempts all 3 negotiation steps calmly 3/5 discussions
activities.generalization: Screen time, privileges, responsibilities discussions
